campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments and money are being managed. They also discussed a plan to improve transportation options around Campbell, including different ways people can get around the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and spending reports
City treasurer's monthly financial update
New multimodal transportation plan (buses, biking, walking, etc.)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to gather community input on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (the formal written rule) and skipping the usual first-reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled to come back for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular meeting
Specific topic of ordinance not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully outlined in the available agenda information.
keyTopics: Approval of multiple March 2026 meeting minutes
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contracts

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming council sessions scheduled for April 21 and April 28, 2026. The council reviewed which items should be added to these agendas, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked on any special notice requirements for documents.
keyTopics: Planning the April 21, 2026 final council agenda
Planning the April 28, 2026 draft council agenda
Setting time limits for specific agenda items
Reviewing public records from early April
VEBA (employee benefits) Advisory Committee discussion

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including which construction and improvement projects to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Capital improvement priorities for 2026-27

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review previous meeting notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would modify how the city handles residential tenant protections.
keyTopics: Tenant protection law changes being proposed
Review of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of the Municipal Code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were invited to speak during public comment time (limited to 3 minutes each). The council also discussed electing a new Chair and Vice Chair for their leadership positions and received a presentation about the upcoming fiscal year budget.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents can address council on any city matter
Election of new Council Chair and Vice Chair
Fiscal Year budget presentation and input

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a City decision and want to sue, you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file your lawsuit, following state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge City decisions in court
Legal rights for residents disputing City rulings
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's housing and community development plans for 2025-2026, new work requests from council members, and potential changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and received a preview of upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Housing and community development plan updates (2025-2030)
Changes to council vacancy filling process
New work priorities requested by individual council members
City Council meeting schedule and administrative approvals

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking the city to officially recognize the building as more historically significant than it currently is, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space if they rehabilitate the building and improve its earthquake safety.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development with historic preservation incentives
